subject: Geography
chapter: Planning and Sustainable Development in Indian Context

questions.q: What is the primary objective of the National Policy of Education (NPE), 1986?
questions.answer: To universalise and improve the quality of education, and to make it more relevant to the needs and aspirations of the Indian society.

questions.q: Which of the following programmes is primarily focused on poverty reduction and social justice?
Explain the concept of 'sustainable development' in the context of Planning in India.
questions.answer: Sustainable development is a pattern of growth in which the needs of the present are met without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. In the context of Planning in India, sustainable development aims to balance economic growth with social and environmental considerations.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The National Commission for Enterprises in the Unorganized Sector (NCEUS) was set up in _______________________.
Which of the following is a key feature of the Planning Commission's approach to planning?
Describe the role of the Gram Panchayat as a key institution in the decentralised planning process.
questions.answer: The Gram Panchayat is a key institution in the decentralised planning process, responsible for identifying the needs of the village, preparing a plan for the village, and implementing the plan with the help of the village community.

questions.q: True or False: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992 made the Gram Panchayat a directly elected body.
Explain the concept of 'inclusive growth' in the context of Planning in India.
questions.answer: Inclusive growth refers to a pattern of growth that benefits all sections of society, particularly the disadvantaged and vulnerable groups. In the context of Planning in India, inclusive growth aims to reduce poverty and inequality, and to promote social justice and equity.

questions.q: Which of the following is a key objective of the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (NREGA)?
questions.answer: To provide a statutory guarantee of wage employment to rural households

questions.q: Describe the role of the Planning Commission in promoting sustainable development in India.
questions.answer: The Planning Commission plays a key role in promoting sustainable development in India by formulating plans and policies that balance economic growth with social and environmental considerations.
